title: Incidence of tuberculosis (per 100,000 people)
definition: Incidence of tuberculosis is the estimated number of new and relapse tuberculosis cases arising in a given year, expressed as the rate per 100,000 population. All forms of TB are included, including cases in people living with HIV. Estimates for all years are recalculated as new information becomes available and techniques are refined, so they may differ from those published previously.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: Global Tuberculosis Report, World Health Organization (WHO)

editorial.shortHook: A disease-burden map with distinctive regional pockets.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Papua New Guinea, Philippines, Lesotho, while the lightest include West Bank and Gaza, Puerto Rico (US), United Arab Emirates; that contrast is the fastest way to read the tuberculosis incidence pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: TB incidence gives players a health-pattern challenge that is not just income by another name.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Safely managed drinking water can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Life expectancy can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Urban slum population can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Distinct disease-burden map that is not just GDP.
Good expert-style Daily with clear source/year.
